lion.description: The Asiatic lion is a majestic big cat found only in the Gir Forest of Gujarat, India.
lion.rescue_guideline: Do not approach. Call Forest Department immediately at 1926. Back away slowly.
lion.interesting_fact: Asiatic lions have smaller manes than African lions and live in small prides.

tiger.description: The Bengal tiger is the national animal of India and one of the largest cats.
tiger.rescue_guideline: Maintain safe distance. Report sighting to forest authorities.
tiger.interesting_fact: Tigers are excellent swimmers and can climb trees when young.

indian cobra.description: Highly venomous snake famous for its hood expansion when threatened.
indian cobra.rescue_guideline: Do not approach or handle. Call professional snake rescuers immediately.
indian cobra.interesting_fact: Responsible for a large number of snakebite deaths in India.

peacock.description: National bird of India known for its iridescent tail feathers.
peacock.habitat: Dry deciduous forests, farmlands, near human settlements
peacock.rescue_guideline: Report injured bird to local forest department.
peacock.interesting_fact: Only males have the long train of feathers.

elephant.description: Largest land mammal in India with high intelligence and complex social structure.
elephant.rescue_guideline: Keep distance if it enters human area. Call forest department.
elephant.interesting_fact: Elephants mourn their dead and have excellent memory.
